Off Calendar Sep-01-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. Y. ANNA SUH OF HUNTON & WILLIAMS, TEL. NO. 1-415-975-3707 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. MARK J. RICE OF THE LAW OFFICES OF MCNEIL, SILVEIRA & WILEY, TEL. NO. 1-415-472-3434 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF/CROSS-DEFENDANT ROBERT HALF INTERNATIONAL, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE DEFENDANTS/CROSS-COMPLAINANTS'CROSS COMPLAINT is denied. Under CCP 436, movant must show the pleading to contain irrelevant, false, or improper matter, the Court finds the Cross-Complaint does not contain irrelevant, false, or improper matter. CCP 475 states: the court must, in every stage of an action, disregard any error, improper ruling, instruction, or defect, in the pleadings or proceedings which, in the opinion of said court, does not affect the substantial rights of the parties. Here, the Court finds Defendant's untimely filing of the cross complaint does not prejudice Plaintiff, further the Court finds Defendant's error resulting in the late filing to be excusable. Thus, Plaintiff's motion to strike is denied. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/eg

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANT BRIAN GABRIELSON'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT, the court finds that California law applies. Ms. Farrugia/Nelson/Klatt made their solicitations while in California and enticed the employees to come to California. California employers have a strong policy in favor of recruiting talented employees to work in California. In addition, plaintiff did not provide any choice of law analysis. The court overrules the demurrer to the breach of contract cause of action. Paragraph 57 of the 2nd Amended Complaint alleges that Gabrielson took plaintiff's documents. Civil Code sec. 3426.7(b) provides that CUTSA does not displace contractual remedies. Business and Professions Code sec. 16600 does not allow an employee to take an employer's property. The court overrules the demurrer as to the UCL cause of action. Plaintiff alleges that all of the defendants (including Ms. Farrugia who allegedly stole trade secrets) acted together in an unlawful and unfair manner. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANT ABRAHAM KLATT'S DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT, the court finds that California law applies. Ms. Farrugia/Nelson/Klatt made their solicitations while in California and enticed the employees to come to California. California employers have a strong policy in favor of recruiting talented employees to work in California. In addition, plaintiff did not provide any choice of law analysis. The court overrules the demurrer to breach of contract cause of action. Plaintiff can allege a breach based on paragraphs 8 and 11 of the contract. Civil Code sec. 3426.7(b) provides that CUTSA does not displace contractual remedies. Plaintiff alleges that Mr. Klatt supervised Ms. Rupp at paragraph 34 of the complaint ad solicited her. A contractual provision preventing a departing employee from "raiding" his former employer's employees is "not void on its face under Business and Professions Code section 16600." Loral Corp. v. Moyes, 174 Cal.App.3d 268, 280 (1985). Fillpoint, LLC v. Mass (2012) 208 Cal.App.4th 1170 is distinguishable because it was a sale of business case. MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-26-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Attys. Y. ANNA SUH (Tel No. 415-975-3700) and ROLAND M. JUAREZ (415-532-2000) of Hunton & Williams appearing for Plaintiff Robert Half International, Inc.;
Attys. MARK J. RICE and JESSICA A. SHAVERS of Law Offices of McNeil, Silveira, Rice & Wiley, Tel No. (415) 472-3434 appearing for Defendants Abraham Klatt, Donna Farrugia, Gary D. Nelson Associates, Inc. d/b/a Nelson Family, and Brian Gabrielson.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. As to DEFENDANT DONNA FARRUGIA AND GARY D NELSON ASSOCIATES, INC. D/B/A NELSON FAMILY'S MOTION TO STRIKE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT, the court grants and denies the motion to strike in part. Ms. Farrugia's notice of motion to strike lists numerous allegations to strike, but does not clearly explain why they are improper. The gist of the motion is to strike the allegations regarding paragraphs 8, 11, and 13 of the contract. The motion is denied as to the paragraph 8 allegations. The covenant to protect the trade secrets is enforceable and does not run afoul of Business and Professions Code sec. 16600. The motion is granted with leave to amend as to the allegations relating to paragraph 11 of the contract [non-solicitation of employee clause]. A contractual provision preventing a departing employee from "raiding" his former employer's employees is "not void on its face under Business and Professions Code section 16600." Loral Corp. v. Moyes, 174 Cal.App.3d 268, 280 (1985). Fillpoint, LLC v. Mass (2012) 208 Cal.App.4th 1170 is distinguishable because it was a sale of business case. Plaintiff's allegations regarding Mr. Gabrielson and Mr. Klatt appear improper. Paragraph 11 prohibits the solicitation of an "Other Employee." The contract defines an "other employee" as working in an "Applicable Office." An "Applicable Office" is one that Ms. Farrugia worked in or had supervisory authority over. Ms. Farrugia did not work with Mr. Gabrielson or Mr. Klatt. The court grants the motion to strike without leave to amend as to the allegations regarding paragraph 13 of the contract [wrongful use]. The covenant seeks to hamper the employees' attempt to engage in their trade and facially violates Business and Professions sec. 16600. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Paula J. Shi, CSR #13358 (302/EHG)
